extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by rule rec_a_from_conjunction (condition: seizure_history AND elderly); otherwise it defaults false. rec_drug_b is set true only by rule rec_b_from_trigger (condition: seizure_history); otherwise it defaults false. These two rules target different outputs, so priorities/ties never cause one to suppress the other — conflict resolution is per-output. The condition for drug a (seizure_history AND elderly) strictly implies the condition for drug b (seizure_history). Therefore any input with seizure_history=true and elderly=true fires both rules: rec_drug_a=true and rec_drug_b=true simultaneously, violating mutual exclusion (rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b). Counterexample: seizure_history=true, elderly=true, asthma=false (asthma only affects rec_drug_c via rec_c_from_asthma and is irrelevant to the property).
